mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server designed to facilitate calls to over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It is ideal for applications requiring AI capabilities with features like cost tracking, load balancing, and logging. Key trade-offs include the need for proper API key management and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools for managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/v1.30.0/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

kubectl version --client

```

> Download the kubectl binary.
> Make the kubectl binary executable.
> Move the binary to your PATH.
> Verify the installation of kubectl.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30.0 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add litellm https://berriai.github.io/litellm

helm install litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.82.1 --namespace litellm --create-namespace

```

> Add the LiteLLM Helm repository.
> Install LiteLLM in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace litellm

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/monitoring.yaml --namespace litellm

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Deploy monitoring resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data.

```bash

helm uninstall litellm --namespace litellm

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the LiteLLM Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up your data, then upgrade LiteLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp litellm-config:/path/to/config backup-config --namespace litellm

helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.82.1 --namespace litellm

kubectl get pods --namespace litellm

```

> Backup your current configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest version of LiteLLM.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): deployments.apps "litellm" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM deployment may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs and ensure the namespace is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the LiteLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your GKE cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM image may not be accessible or the tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your Helm values file.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs, improved logging features, and enhanced load balancing capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace litellm'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
